summary: Matched a cluster of previously NOT_IMPLEMENTED functions in src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c, mainly common fighter Entry/EntryStart/EntryEnd behavior and Leadead capture cleanup. The PR also refined ftCommon motion-variable layout, ftCommonData field types, Player_GetUnk4C return type, Leadead item prototype, and added motion-state enum offset comments. decomp.dev reported 16 new matches and +4172 bytes of matched code.
what_changed: Implemented ftCo_800C61B0 entry setup: reads Player_GetUnk4C, stores original JObj scale, temporarily sets y scale from p_ftCommonData->x6C4, changes to ftCo_MS_Entry, sets invisibility and fighter flags, and initializes an ftCollisionBox.

Implemented ftCo_800C6408 EntryStart transition: sets entry timer from p_ftCommonData->x6BC, changes to ftCo_MS_EntryStart, configures accessory trophy scale/rotation/translation when x221F_b4 is false, installs fn_800C69F4 as accessory callback, spawns effect 0x43E, plays audio 0x8B, and triggers ftCo_800BFFD0 with 0x75.

Implemented EntryStart and EntryEnd physics/collision functions using entry motion-vars for scale interpolation, vertical offset x28, accessory scaling, floor/air collision helpers ft_80083E64 and ft_800846B0, and Player_GetEntityAtIndex for linked player/entity behavior.

Added accessory update callbacks fn_800C69F4 and fn_800C6F34, including logic to follow another entity's y position when needed and update accessory translation with a local fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate helper.
Implemented ftCo_800C6B6C EntryEnd transition: resets JObj scale to saved entry.x8, adjusts y position, changes to ftCo_MS_EntryEnd with flags 0x3000, and installs fn_800C6F34.
Matched ftCo_800C70D0 and ftCo_800C7178 special-case motion transitions for Mario/Luigi to Wait and WalkSlow, while non-matching fighter kinds fall back through inlineB2/ftCo_800C7070 behavior.

Changed Kirby-specific branches in ftCo_800C7220, ftCo_800C7294, and ftCo_800C7308 from shared ftCo_800C7070 calls to explicit Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls for Wait, WalkSlow, and WalkMiddle plus x2219 flag writes.

Implemented ftCo_CaptureLeadead_Anim to decrement grab_timer by p_ftCommonData->x734, call ftCommon_8007DC08 with x738, release the Leadead item via it_802EAE80 when the timer expires, and enter ftCo_800C7800.

Implemented ftCo_800C7800 as a typed void function taking Fighter_GObj*, applying knockback/cut velocity from p_ftCommonData->x370 based on ground_or_air, changing to ftCo_MS_CaptureCut, and setting capture_timer from p_ftCommonData->x73C.
Extended ftCommon_MotionVars.entry with x4, x8, x14, x20, x24, x28, and x2C fields at documented fp offsets.
Refined ftCommonData fields x6BC and x6C0 from UNK_T to int, documented x734 and x738 as Leadead capture timer decrement and grab break threshold, and changed x73C to int.
Changed Player_GetUnk4C return type from u8 to int in player.c/player.h and changed it_802EAE80 prototype to take HSD_GObj*.
Added explicit hex offset comments for selected ftCo_MS_* enum values, including early dead/wait/walk states, CaptureCut, Entry/EntryStart/EntryEnd, and CaptureCrazyHand.
smart_moves: Matched behavior by first exposing the missing motion-variable storage in ftCommon_MotionVars.entry, making the implementation readable and avoiding raw offset access.

Used header/prototype refinement to unlock cleaner function bodies: Player_GetUnk4C returning int, ftCo_800C7800 taking Fighter_GObj*, it_802EAE80 taking HSD_GObj*, and ftCommonData fields typed as int/float.

Preserved matching-sensitive codegen with explicit inline wrapper chains around ftCo_800C6AFC and a fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate that reproduces HSD_JObjSetTranslate-style asserts and matrix-dirty behavior.
Kept motion-state enum documentation close to the enum definitions, making future decompilation of Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls easier to audit.
Handled linked/player clone behavior through Player_GetEntityAtIndex branches instead of assuming only the current fighter controls y position and collision-bottom values.
For tiny 99.79% near-matches, replaced shared helper calls with explicit Fighter_ChangeMotionState plus flag writes where that matched the original codegen for Kirby branches.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter action code, check whether ftCommon_MotionVars has enough named/offset-correct storage before forcing expressions into unrelated common fields.
Field type precision in shared data tables matters for codegen: p_ftCommonData timer fields used as int in divisions and capture_timer fields used as int should not remain UNK_T.

If an SDK inline such as HSD_JObjSetTranslate does not inline exactly, a local fake inline with the same asserts, __FILE__ identity, and matrix update call can be a valid temporary matching tactic, but it should be marked with a TODO when the proper inlining story is unresolved.
Small wrapper inlines can be used deliberately to influence whether a helper call is emitted directly or through nested inline expansion.
Entry/respawn-style fighter code may need to coordinate model scale, accessory scale, cur_pos.y, collision-box bottom, invisibility, and fighter bitflags as one matched unit.

For capture states, animation callbacks can own timer decrement, item release, break-threshold updates, and transition into a cut/release motion state; all related constants should be typed/documented in ftCommonData.
Enum offset comments are useful evidence when many Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls use common-state IDs such as ftCo_MS_Entry, ftCo_MS_CaptureCut, ftCo_MS_Wait, and ftCo_MS_WalkSlow.
naming_conventions: Common fighter motion states use the ftCo_MS_* prefix, with selected entries documented as /* 0xNNN */ ftCo_MS_Name.
Common fighter functions in this file use ftCo_800Cxxxx for address-based names when semantic names are not known, while callbacks without semantic names use fn_800Cxxxx.
Unknown motion-variable fields remain xN names but are placed under semantic sub-structs such as mv.co.entry and mv.co.captureleadead with fp offset comments.
Shared ftCommonData unknown fields retain x-offset names but can gain type refinements and short Doxygen comments when usage is understood.
Function prototypes should replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once call sites prove return type and argument types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in EntryStart_Coll and EntryEnd_Coll to preserve stack shape.
Introduced fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate with #undef/#define __FILE__ "jobj.h" and HSD_ASSERT line numbers 916 and 917 to match SDK inline/assert codegen.
Added ftCo_800C6AFC_please_dontinline and ftCo_800C6AFC_dontinline wrapper chain to control inlining around the matrix update path.
Used nested inlineB0/inlineB1/inlineB2 wrappers to preserve call/inline structure around ftCo_800C7070 fallback paths.

Kept temporary-style variable names such as temp_r31, temp_f31, sp20, and sp48 in larger matched functions, indicating code was shaped around compiler output rather than fully cleaned semantic naming.
Special-cased simple fighter-kind branches explicitly for Mario, Luigi, Kirby, and Giga Bowser where helper sharing did not produce matching output.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 44.54% (+0.11%, +4172 bytes) with 16 new matches, all in main/melee/ft/ft_0C31.

The bot listed full matches for ftCo_800C6408, ftCo_EntryStart_Phys, ftCo_800C61B0, ftCo_800C6B6C, ftCo_EntryEnd_Phys, fn_800C69F4, fn_800C6F34, ftCo_EntryStart_Coll, ftCo_EntryEnd_Coll, ftCo_800C7800, ftCo_800C70D0, ftCo_800C7178, ftCo_CaptureLeadead_Anim, ftCo_800C7220, ftCo_800C7294, and ftCo_800C7308.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replaced fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate or resolved the TODO about proper HSD_JObjSetTranslate inlining.
Search for later semantic naming of ftCommon_MotionVars.entry fields x4, x8, x14, x20, x24, x28, and x2C.
Search for later semantic naming of ftCommonData fields x6BC, x6C0, x6C4, x734, x738, and x73C.
Find PRs touching ftCo_800C78B0 and nearby CaptureLikelike code after ftCo_800C7800 was typed.
Search for other PRs using wrapper inline chains to force matching around SDK JObj helpers.
